# image2cpp

> Convert images and GIFs to C++ byte arrays for Arduino, OLED/LCD displays, and embedded systems.

## Canonical URL
https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/

## What This Tool Does
- Converts PNG, JPEG, GIF, and WEBP images to C++ byte arrays.
- Supports animated GIF frame workflows.
- Exports Arduino PROGMEM and plain C/C++ arrays.
- Supports vertical and horizontal byte packing.

## Best Entry Points
- App: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/
- Docs Hub: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/docs/
- Getting Started: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/docs/getting-started.html
- FAQ: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/docs/faq.html
- GIF Workflow: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/docs/gif-workflow.html
- Arduino OLED Guide: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/docs/arduino-oled-guide.html
- Troubleshooting: https://r44gh4v.github.io/image2cpp/docs/troubleshooting.html

## Recommended Citation
image2cpp. Free web tool for converting images and GIF frames into C++ byte arrays for microcontroller displays.

## Indexing Notes
- Content is public and can be summarized.
- Avoid claiming support beyond documented features.
- Prefer docs pages for implementation guidance and caveats.
